candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: Evidence is strongest for new-home sales workflows and adjacent residential lead handling; it does not by itself prove Avito can reduce paid traffic spend.
candidate_theses.statement: A credible path to lower paid-traffic dependence is to improve conversion from existing demand: AI sales agents and workflow tools can engage neglected leads, infer intent, and hand better-qualified buyers to human operators.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: Most evidence comes from agent CRM and seller/buyer workflows rather than developer-specific CPA products.
candidate_theses.statement: First-party CRM, conversation, and behavioral data are becoming actionable inputs for lead scoring, prioritization, and follow-up, which supports the CDP/intent-model part of the ND hypothesis.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: The articles support property and listing intelligence generally; direct evidence for new-development lot-level availability and developer inventory feeds is weaker.
candidate_theses.statement: Better matching depends on structured property data and richer listing content, including AI search, contact enrichment, property reports, exterior/site-plan context, and listing-level assistants.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: The monetization evidence is mostly portal/listing-agent economics, not quality-based CPA for developers; this should be framed as an analogue, not proof.
candidate_theses.statement: There is an analogue for monetizing lead quality through differentiated portal economics: Homes.com emphasizes listing-agent leads and paid memberships, while AI lead-routing examples point toward paying for better-qualified demand rather than raw impressions.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: The evidence supports human handoff and augmentation, but does not quantify the optimal operator model or economics.
candidate_theses.statement: The strongest product pattern is AI-assisted human handoff, not full automation: agents/operators remain important for complex decisions, trust, and conversion moments.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: Risk evidence comes from broader AI outreach and agent commentary, not a direct ND developer CPA case.
candidate_theses.statement: Automated lead activation creates legal, consent, and trust risks, especially when outreach uses synthetic voice or opaque automation.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: This is a cautionary thesis; it should not be used to reject the whole hypothesis, only to bound it.
candidate_theses.statement: AI can raise productivity but may underperform in emotionally complex, high-trust, or brand-sensitive real estate interactions, so the benchmark should expect caveats about over-automation.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: This is useful context for content quality, but only indirectly connected to CPA or lead monetization.
candidate_theses.statement: Richer visual and property-context data can improve buyer confidence and recommendation quality, but the evidence is indirect for ND CPA monetization.

candidate_theses.evidence_limitations: This article is intentionally a close distractor: it discusses AI and marketing measurement but should not anchor a core ND investment thesis.
candidate_theses.statement: General AI marketing advice and brand voice guidance are too broad to validate data-driven CPA for new developments.
